violations.finding: Executable bash snippet in command file: CLOSE_REPORT="$(bash ~/.claude/scripts/resolve-close-report.sh "$TASK_ID")" || true — a command-substitution block that runs a shell command inline
violations.recommendation: Rewrite as prose instruction referencing the script, or extract to a named helper script that encapsulates the full close-report resolution + error-handling flow

violations.finding: Executable bash block in command file: 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' — an inline script invocation inside a ```bash code block
violations.recommendation: Rewrite as prose instruction or consolidate into a wrapper script that handles venv activation and script dispatch

violations.finding: Executable bash block in command file: 'source venv/bin/activate && python /root/.claude/scripts/write-commit-grant.py --task-id "$TASK_ID"' — inline script invocation in a ```bash code block
violations.recommendation: Rewrite as prose instruction or consolidate venv activation into a wrapper script

violations.finding: Multi-line executable bash block in command file: 'source venv/bin/activate && python "${CONTROL_ROOT}/scripts/write-commit-grant.py" \ --task-id "$TASK_ID" \ --revoke-existing-for-task "$TASK_ID"' — a multi-line shell command, which Standard 1 explicitly lists as a violation
violations.recommendation: Extract the revoke-and-refresh grant flow into a dedicated helper script (e.g. scripts/refresh-commit-grant.sh) and reference it by name in prose

standards_passed: use-source-venv (Standard 3): no bare python3 calls in any .sh or .md file in changed set
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4): no decimal step numbering found in changed .md files
meaningful-naming (Standard 5): no forbidden naming patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2) in changed files

english-only (Standard 6): unconditional scan of agents/*.md and commands/*.md found only the exempted mascot rank labels in agents/style-inspector.md:333 (Cycle-2 exemption per spec-20260518-225715 §5.1 applies); no violations in changed files

merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7): scripts/step7-spec-update.py implements a distinct algorithm (Step 7 selection + STEP7_* trace logic); no overlap with spec-check.py (cp-state management), write-commit-grant.py (grant writing), or resolve-close-report.sh (path resolution)
concise-documentation (Standard 8): commands/commit.md contains no marketing language, no repetitive examples, no story-style text; length driven by algorithmic precision of Step 7 staged algorithm

no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2): scripts/step7-spec-update.py has 'specs_dir = dev_docs_root / "specs"' where dev_docs_root is a required --dev-docs-root CLI parameter; 'specs' is an algorithmic constant mandated by commit.md, not an environment-specific value

parameterized-scripts (Standard 9): scripts/step7-spec-update.py accepts all variable values via argparse (--task-id required, --dev-docs-root required, --bulk, --dryrun, --changelog-status, --push-gate-token-path)

dangling-references (Standard 10): all script references verified — resolve-close-report.sh, step7-spec-update.py, write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, write-commit-grant.py, aggregate-dev-report.py, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh all exist on disk

merge-conflicts (Standard 11): no actual merge conflict markers (<<<<<<< HEAD / ======= / >>>>>>> branch) found in any changed file; detect-merge-conflicts.sh reported INDEX.md as false positive due to grep pattern '=======$' matching '============================================================================' description lines in file tree listings

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex flagged 4 issues: (1) Standard 1 under-classification — commands/commit.md:152-156 is a real multi-line shell command violation and commands/commit.md:53-55 is an executable command-substitution snippet that was omitted from draft; (2) Standard 11 false-positive wording — confirmed no real conflict markers, suggested cleaner tooling-bug characterization; (3) Standard 10 verification — incomplete citation of checked references (aggregate-dev-report.py, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh also verified); (4) Standard 11 scope — detector findings outside the changed-files list should not become findings in a --changed-files audit.

findings.issue: commands/commit.md:152-156 is a real multi-line shell command violation (not merely borderline), and commands/commit.md:53-55 was omitted — it is an executable command-substitution snippet. Both qualify as Standard 1 violations per explicit rule language.
findings.proposed_fix: Add commands/commit.md:53-55 as a Standard 1 violation (command-substitution snippet). Upgrade commands/commit.md:152-156 from 'borderline' to 'critical violation' (multi-line bash command, explicitly listed in Standard 1). Keep 80-82 and 88-90 as violations.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct: Standard 1 explicitly lists 'Multi-line shell commands' as violations, and command-substitution snippets executing scripts are executable code. All four bash blocks are now reported as violations.

findings.issue: Standard 11 false-positive wording: detect-merge-conflicts.sh uses '=======$' pattern which matches end-of-line separator descriptions in INDEX.md tree listings, not actual merge conflict markers.
findings.proposed_fix: Note the detector bug in the standards_passed entry rather than as an advisory finding; no INDEX.md merge conflict violation should be raised.
findings.rationale: Handled in standards_passed entry for Standard 11, which explicitly documents the false positive mechanism and confirms no actual markers exist.

findings.issue: Standard 10 verification citation was incomplete — did not list aggregate-dev-report.py, score-inject.sh, score-update.sh which are also referenced in changed files.
findings.proposed_fix: Expand standards_passed entry to enumerate all verified scripts including aggregate-dev-report.py, score-inject.sh, and score-update.sh.
findings.rationale: Updated the standards_passed entry for Standard 10 to include all verified references.

findings.issue: Standard 11 detector output includes files outside the changed-files list (hooks/*.md, scripts/check-file-references.sh, etc.) which should not become findings in a --changed-files scoped audit.
findings.proposed_fix: Filter merge-conflict detector findings to the supplied changed-files set before reporting. Standard 6 is the only documented unconditional-scan exception.
findings.rationale: Only INDEX.md (which is in the changed-files list) was assessed for Standard 11; other files reported by the detector are out of scope for this --changed-files invocation.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Added commands/commit.md:53-55 as a Standard 1 violation (command-substitution snippet). Upgraded all four bash blocks to critical violations (including the multi-line block at 152-156). Expanded Standard 10 reference list. Standard 11 filtered to changed-files scope only.
